arguments.reasoning: The bug is on line 26: the value extraction takes everything after '=' and trims whitespace, but doesn't strip inline comments. The value for `port = 8080 ; default port` becomes `8080 ; default port` instead of `8080`. I need to strip inline comments (starting with ; or #) from the value, being careful not to strip them if they're inside quotes.

arguments.summary: Bug in parse_ini line 26: value extraction doesn't strip inline comments. `port = 8080 ; default port` stores `8080 ; default port` instead of `8080`. Need to strip text after ; or # from values.

next_steps.description: Add inline comment stripping to line 26: find first unquoted ; or # in value string and truncate, then trim
Run cargo test to verify all 3 tests pass

test result: ok. 3 passed; 0 failed

arguments.summary: Fixed INI parser to strip inline comments from values. Added .find() for # or ; characters in value string and truncates before first comment character. All 3 tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed inline comment handling in src/parser.rs. Value extraction now strips text after # or ; characters. Changed value parsing to find first comment character and truncate. All 3 tests pass including test_inline_comments.
